top_signals.title: From Pyongyang to Primorsk: When Sanctions Evasion Becomes System Design
top_signals.summary:
Rarely a week passes without a new story about Russia’s shadow fleet. Tankers catch fire in the Mediterranean, are added to sanctions lists, or are boarded while passing through European waters. But the bigger story is not the vessels that are caught, but those that aren’t — ships moving between registries, ports, shell companies, and service providers that obscure their ties to Russia while keeping a sanctioned state afloat. The vessels that do get sanctioned are the visible tip of a larger scheme that North Korea spent years running, and Russia has refined at scale.Shadow fleets are typically studied in

The post From Pyongyang to Primorsk: When Sanctions Evasion Becomes System Design appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Tech Retreat Deepens as Traders Eye CPI Spike: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: The retreat in technology stocks extended further as traders pared exposure ahead of data that is expected to show US inflation has hit the highest level in more than three years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Banks Pay 7% on Dollar Deposits as India Seeks Fresh Foreign Currency
top_signals.summary: Indian lenders have sharply raised rates on foreign-currency deposits to lure the nation’s overseas residents, offering an early test of the central bank’s recent measures to bring in capital flows to support the rupee.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Bonnie Chan on IPO Momentum
top_signals.summary: Bonnie Chan, CEO, Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing Ltd. discusses Hong Kong’s IPO pipeline explaining issuers and investors a lot more engaged than in previous years. She was speaking to Bloomberg’s David Ingles at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Indian families scale back on gold for weddings as prices hover near highs
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Edaa on Market Infrastructure and Capital Flows
top_signals.summary: Hanan Mohammad AlShehri, CEO, Securities Depository Center Company (Edaa), Saudi Tadawul Group discusses opening Saudi Arabia’s capital markets to foreign investors with Bloomberg’s Lulu Chen at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)
